description:
h2. Epic Goal
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* push webhooks across every supported provider plus Stripe, so my data stays fresh automatically,
*so that* an upstream push marks my SBOM stale and a payment event updates billing without me lifting a finger.

h3. Scope
GitHub HMAC-SHA256, Codeberg/Forgejo/Gitea, Bitbucket (no HMAC), and Stripe signature-verified webhooks; registration + health endpoints; E2E coverage.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a verified push webhook, *when* received, *then* the SBOM is marked stale and a {{webhook_sbom_stale}} audit entry is written.
* *Given* a bad signature, *when* received, *then* it returns 401.
* *Given* the webhook E2E/registration/health suites, *then* they pass.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-159 GitHub HMAC
* CRAN-160 Codeberg/Forgejo/Gitea
* CRAN-161 Bitbucket (no HMAC)
* CRAN-162 Stripe signature verification
* CRAN-163 E2E + registration + health

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* GitHub push events to mark my SBOM stale, verified by HMAC,
*so that* my dependency inventory refreshes automatically and only genuine GitHub pushes are trusted.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{webhooks/github-webhook.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* it passes.
* *Given* an HMAC mismatch, *when* received, *then* it returns 401.

h3. Implementation Notes
HMAC-SHA256 against {{GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET}}; marks SBOM stale; daily 2 AM auto-sync.

h3. Sources
Commit {{ceddd7c}} (2026-02-21, webhook concern).

h3. User Story
*As a* self-hosted-provider customer,
*I want* Codeberg/Forgejo/Gitea push webhooks,
*so that* self-hosted repos trigger the same stale-SBOM refresh as GitHub.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{webhooks/codeberg-webhook.test.ts}}, *when* run against local Forgejo, *then* it passes.
* *Given* {{routes/webhook-e2e.test.ts}} B5/B6, *then* they pass.

h3. Implementation Notes
Reuses HMAC where supported, else the 'repo tracked in Neo4j' check.

h3. Sources
Provider-expansion commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* Bitbucket customer,
*I want* Bitbucket push webhooks (which carry no HMAC),
*so that* Bitbucket pushes refresh my SBOM, verified by repo-tracking knowledge.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a Bitbucket {{X-Event-Key: repo:push}}, *when* received, *then* the 'tracked in Neo4j' verification runs and the stale flow fires.

h3. Implementation Notes
Defence by knowledge (no HMAC available).

h3. Sources
Session 59 Bitbucket commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* Stripe webhooks verified against the signing secret,
*so that* billing lifecycle events are processed only when genuinely from Stripe.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{webhooks/stripe-webhook.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* signature-pass + signature-fail are covered.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{stripe.webhooks.constructEvent}} against {{STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET}}; handles subscription/invoice/customer events.

h3. Sources
Stripe billing commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* webhook registration, an E2E flow and a health endpoint,
*so that* I can subscribe/unsubscribe webhooks and see last-received-at per webhook.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/webhook-e2e.test.ts}}, {{webhook-registration.test.ts}}, {{webhook-health.test.ts}}, {{push-events.test.ts}}, *then* all pass.

h3. Implementation Notes
Subscribe/unsubscribe on the provider; health reports last-received-at.

h3. Sources
P2 #12 webhook E2E commits.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* compliance officer / manufacturer,
*I want* cross-product overviews of stakeholders, contributors, dependencies and repos,
*so that* I can see and act on each dimension across my whole portfolio rather than product-by-product.

h3. Scope
Stakeholders page (org + product CRA/NIS2 roles); contributors, dependencies and repos cross-product overviews.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* each overview, *when* loaded, *then* its {{*-overview.test.ts}} suite passes.
* *Given* the repos overview, *when* rendered, *then* 6 provider tabs show with badge counts.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-164 Stakeholders
* CRAN-165 Contributors
* CRAN-166 Dependencies
* CRAN-167 Repos

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* a stakeholders page for org- and product-level CRA/NIS2 roles,
*so that* the right contacts are recorded for each compliance responsibility, editable in place.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/stakeholders.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* list + inline update are covered.
* *Given* the {{stakeholders}} table, *when* first accessed, *then* org + product rows auto-create.

h3. Implementation Notes
Org: manufacturer/auth-rep/compliance officer. Product: security contact/tech-file owner/IR lead.

h3. Sources
Commit {{a4f8a45}} (2026-02-22, stakeholders concern).

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* a cross-product contributor overview,
*so that* I can see who contributes across all my connected repos.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/contributors-overview.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* it passes.

h3. Implementation Notes
Per-contributor product count, last commit, languages.

h3. Sources
Commit {{fadff46}} (2026-02-22).

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* a cross-product dependency overview,
*so that* I can see aggregate dependency counts, ecosystems and the most vulnerable packages.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/dependencies-overview.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* it passes.

h3. Implementation Notes
Direct + transitive counts, ecosystems, top vulnerable packages.

h3. Sources
Commit {{fadff46}}.

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* a cross-product repos overview with provider tabs,
*so that* I can see and sync every connected repo from one page.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/repos-overview.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* it passes.
* *Given* the page, *when* rendered, *then* 6 provider tabs show (GH/Codeberg/Gitea/Forgejo/GitLab/Bitbucket).

h3. Implementation Notes
Provider filter tabs with badge counts, last-sync state, sync-now action.

h3. Sources
Commit {{fadff46}}.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* platform owner / customer,
*I want* Stripe-backed subscriptions with contributor/product pricing, tiers, trials and a billing gate,
*so that* the platform monetises fairly, restricts non-paying writes without locking people out, and pricing is configurable without redeploy.

h3. Scope
Contributor-based Standard tier + 90-day trial + Stripe portal; global billing-gate middleware; Pro tier (€9/product + €6/contributor) with {{requirePlan}}; admin-configurable pricing in {{platform_settings}}; admin trial/exemption/pause; billing-tier docs.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/billing.test.ts}} + {{webhooks/stripe-webhook.test.ts}}, *then* they pass; E2E {{billing-and-subscription.spec.ts}} passes.
* *Given* a restricted org, *when* it writes, *then* the gate blocks it while reads continue.
* *Given* {{requirePlan('pro')}}, *when* a standard org hits a Pro route, *then* it returns 402.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-168 Standard tier baseline
* CRAN-169 Billing-gate middleware
* CRAN-170 Pro tier
* CRAN-171 Configurable pricing
* CRAN-172 Billing-tier docs
* CRAN-173 Admin trial/exemption/pause

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* customer,
*I want* a contributor-based Standard subscription with a 90-day trial,
*so that* I can pay fairly per active contributor and try the platform free first.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/billing.test.ts}} + {{webhooks/stripe-webhook.test.ts}}, *then* they pass.
* *Given* {{org_billing}}, *when* inspected, *then* trial fields exist.
* *Given* E2E {{billing-and-subscription.spec.ts}}, *then* it passes.

h3. Implementation Notes
€6/contributor/month; Stripe customer portal; trial lifecycle + grace + Resend notices; 4 AM scheduler checks.

h3. Sources
Commit {{8987f8a}} (2026-02-25 10:15).

h3. User Story
*As a* platform owner,
*I want* a global billing gate that blocks writes for restricted orgs,
*so that* non-paying orgs can't mutate data but can still log in to fix their billing.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{security/billing-gate.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* blocked-write + allowed-read are covered.
* *Given* {{integration/billing-gate-enforcement.test.ts}}, *then* it passes end-to-end.

h3. Implementation Notes
Express middleware on write routes; {{status='restricted'}} blocks writes, reads continue.

h3. Sources
Commit {{8987f8a}} (billing-gate concern).

h3. User Story
*As a* product-heavy customer,
*I want* a Pro tier priced per product + contributor,
*so that* orgs with many products get a fair tier that unlocks premium capabilities.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{requirePlan('pro')}}, *when* exported, *then* it enforces standard<pro<enterprise.
* *Given* a Pro route, *when* hit by a standard org, *then* it returns 402.

h3. Implementation Notes
€9/product + €6/contributor; gates Trust Centre publish, public API v1, AI Copilot.

h3. Sources
Pro-tier introduction commit.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform admin,
*I want* pricing values editable without a redeploy,
*so that* I can change pricing from the admin panel.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{platform_settings}}, *when* inspected, *then* it is {{key text pk, value jsonb, updated_at}}.
* *Given* {{/admin/system}}, *when* opened, *then* pricing controls are exposed.

h3. Implementation Notes
Key-value pricing store.

h3. Sources
Pro-tier + admin commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform owner,
*I want* the tier model and pricing rationale documented before Stripe go-live,
*so that* the billing design is captured for due diligence.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docs/billing-tier.md}}, *when* checked, *then* it exists at HEAD.

h3. Implementation Notes
Pre-Stripe documentation.

h3. Sources
Commit {{4dd6841}} (2026-02-25, billing-tier doc concern).

h3. User Story
*As a* platform admin,
*I want* controls to extend trials, exempt, or pause billing for an org,
*so that* I can manage edge-case billing situations without code changes.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/admin-orgs.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* the three actions are covered.
* *Given* any action, *when* taken, *then* an audit-log entry is written.

h3. Implementation Notes
Extend trial / exempt / pause set {{trial_ends_at}} / {{status}}; respected by the gate.

h3. Sources
Stripe + admin-orgs commits.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* manufacturer (and prospective buyer of their software),
*I want* a public Trust Centre where I publish live, data-derived compliance badges and can be contacted,
*so that* buyers can discover and trust my compliance posture, derived from real evidence rather than self-attestation.

h3. Scope
Public compliance directory (profiles + contact log + rate limiting); landing/UI polish; codebase-wide Marketplace→Trust Centre rename (table name retained); live badge derivation; Pro-gating on publish.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/trust-centre.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* list/detail/contact are covered.
* *Given* underlying data changes, *when* badges recompute, *then* they update within seconds.
* *Given* the publish endpoint, *when* a standard org hits it, *then* {{requirePlan('pro')}} returns 402.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-174 Marketplace baseline
* CRAN-175 Landing + UI polish
* CRAN-176 Trust Centre rename
* CRAN-177 Badge derivation
* CRAN-178 Pro-gating

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* a public compliance directory with searchable profiles and gated contact,
*so that* buyers can find my product and contact me, while I control my listing.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/trust-centre.test.ts}} (renamed from {{marketplace.test.ts}}), *when* run, *then* list/detail/contact are covered.
* *Given* the categories, *when* listed, *then* 10 are present (IoT, Industrial, Automotive, Healthcare, FinTech, Enterprise, Open Source, SaaS, Cybersecurity, Other).

h3. Implementation Notes
{{marketplace_profiles}} + {{marketplace_contact_log}}; {{computeComplianceBadges()}}; public list/detail + auth-gated contact (3/day, 1/org/7d, self-contact blocked); settings page to toggle/edit.

h3. Sources
Commit {{d235d5c}} (2026-02-25 10:53).

h3. User Story
*As a* visitor,
*I want* a polished landing page and full-width directory layout,
*so that* the public face of the product reads cleanly and professionally.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{/}} and {{/marketplace}}, *when* viewed, *then* the hero ('Compliance without the chaos'), alignment and full-width card list match the intended design.

h3. Implementation Notes
Card layout switched tiled→full-width; accent-blue logo '2' outside admin.

h3. Sources
Commit {{62fec94}} (2026-02-25 13:29).

h3. User Story
*As a* engineer,
*I want* every code-level 'Marketplace' reference renamed to 'Trust Centre',
*so that* the product's naming is consistent without ever renaming a customer-data table.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a repo grep for 'marketplace' (excluding scratch/evidence/backups), *when* run, *then* only the {{marketplace_profiles}} table name + acceptable docs remain.
* *Given* the 90+ renamed tests, *then* they pass.

h3. Implementation Notes
Routes/services/pages/tests/e2e/docs/help renamed; table name retained per CLAUDE.md rule 14 (no renaming customer-data tables).

h3. Sources
Session 60 rename commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* buyer evaluating a supplier,
*I want* compliance badges derived from live data, not self-attestation,
*so that* the badges I see reflect the supplier's actual current evidence.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/trust-classification.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* it passes.
* *Given* underlying data changes, *when* recomputed, *then* the badge updates within seconds.

h3. Implementation Notes
CRA-ready / NIS2-ready / IP-attested / SBOM-published from obligations + tech-file + scan freshness + IP-proof existence.

h3. Sources
Commit {{d235d5c}} + session-60 refresh.

h3. User Story
*As a* Pro-plan manufacturer,
*I want* Trust Centre publishing gated to the Pro plan,
*so that* publishing is a Pro capability, consistently gated like the other premium suites.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* E2E {{trust-centre.spec.ts}}, *when* run on a fresh stack, *then* it passes (org upgraded to Pro in beforeAll, restored in afterAll).
* *Given* the publish endpoint, *when* a standard org hits it, *then* {{requirePlan('pro')}} returns 402.

h3. Implementation Notes
Mirrors the public-api-v1 E2E pattern.

h3. Sources
Session 63 E2E fix {{c2e1318}}.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* manufacturer (and their customers),
*I want* an EU-sovereign software-escrow orchestrator that deposits compliance artefacts to a self-hosted Forgejo,
*so that* my customers have assured access to my source + evidence under escrow, with daily deposits and EU data residency.

h3. Scope
Self-hosted Forgejo v10 (escrow.cranis2.dev) with 7 artefact types, per-product toggles and daily 5 AM deposits; escrow-agent collaborator access; final deposit on product deletion; repo-URL gating; accordion sidebar.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/escrow.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* config/setup/deposit/deposits/status (6 endpoints) are covered.
* *Given* an escrow agent, *when* invited, *then* a Forgejo collaborator is created (re-invite does not reset the password).
* *Given* product deletion, *when* it runs, *then* a final {{product_deleted}} deposit is made and the repo is preserved.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-179 Orchestrator baseline
* CRAN-180 Agent access
* CRAN-181 Final deposit on delete
* CRAN-182 Repo-URL gating
* CRAN-183 Accordion sidebar

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* a Forgejo-backed escrow orchestrator with daily artefact deposits,
*so that* my compliance artefacts are escrowed automatically in an EU-sovereign store.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/escrow.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* config/setup/deposit/deposits/status are covered.
* *Given* the Tunnel, *when* checked, *then* Forgejo is reachable at {{escrow.cranis2.dev}}.

h3. Implementation Notes
Forgejo v10 in Docker + Cloudflare Tunnel; 7 artefact types; per-product toggles; daily 5 AM deposits with date/hour gating; {{escrow_configs}} + {{escrow_deposits}}.

h3. Sources
Commit {{f3835b2}} (2026-02-26 10:21).

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* to grant an escrow agent collaborator access to my Forgejo repo,
*so that* a nominated escrow agent can access the escrowed material, tracked per engagement.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/escrow.test.ts}}, *when* run, *then* agent invite/re-invite/multi-product are covered.
* *Given* a first invite, *when* sent, *then* a Forgejo collaborator is created; *given* a re-invite, *then* the password is not reset.

h3. Implementation Notes
One-time credentials banner (new) / access notice (existing); agent reference codes; Resend emails; multi-product agent support.

h3. Sources
Commit {{27152d4}} (2026-02-26, escrow-agent concern).

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer leaving the platform,
*I want* a final escrow deposit fired on product deletion,
*so that* the escrow holds a complete final snapshot and the repo survives as a permanent archive.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the delete flow, *when* run, *then* a final {{product_deleted}} deposit precedes cascade-delete (covered by escrow/integration test).
* *Given* the cascade, *when* complete, *then* the Forgejo repo remains.

h3. Implementation Notes
Customer can always return to the archive.

h3. Sources
Commit {{27152d4}} (product-deletion concern).

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* escrow setup gated on a repo URL existing,
*so that* I'm clearly told escrow needs a repo, and products without one still delete/export fine.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a product without {{repoUrl}}, *when* opening escrow setup, *then* a friendly gating message shows.

h3. Implementation Notes
Delete/export handles repo-less products.

h3. Sources
Commit {{27152d4}} (gating concern).

h3. User Story
*As a* user navigating the app,
*I want* an accordion sidebar that keeps navigation tidy,
*so that* only the relevant menu section is open and I can see where I am.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the sidebar, *when* used, *then* it collapses cleanly and auto-expands the section matching the current route (verified on hard refresh).

h3. Implementation Notes
Chevron indicators; one section open at a time; active dot when a collapsed section holds the current page.

h3. Sources
Commit {{27152d4}} (accordion-sidebar concern).
